title: Fix timer cleanup race condition in DistributedModule
description: The handlePlayPause function creates an interval and stores it in timerRef. If the component re-renders between setPlaying(true) and setInterval assignment, timerRef.current is briefly null while playing is true, potentially leaking timers. Fix by using useEffect to manage interval lifecycle tied to the playing state.
acceptanceCriteria: Timer is managed via useEffect tied to playing state
No timer leaks under rapid play/pause toggling
Timer cleans up properly on component unmount

title: Fix handleCrashNode reading stale crashedNodes from closure
description: The handleCrashNode callback has crashedNodes in its dependency array but reads clusterRef.current.getState() which may have already processed the crash. This can lead to trying to crash an already-crashed node in rapid succession. Use functional state update pattern or read current state via ref.
acceptanceCriteria: Double-clicking Crash Node crashes two different nodes
crashedNodes state is always current when handleCrashNode executes

title: Add displayName to all memo() components in visualization directory
description: Several memo() wrapped components in the visualization directory lack displayName, making React DevTools debugging harder. Add explicit displayName or use named function pattern consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: All memo() components in src/components/visualization/ have displayName
React DevTools shows proper component names for all visualization components

title: Fix onAlgoChange callback not passed to AlgorithmPanel
description: AlgorithmSidebar receives onAlgoChange prop but never passes it to the AlgorithmPanel child component. Algorithm selection changes in the sidebar are not propagated back to the parent module state.
acceptanceCriteria: onAlgoChange prop passed through AlgorithmSidebar to AlgorithmPanel
Algorithm selection in sidebar updates the properties panel's displayed algorithm info

title: Fix edgeType property access without fallback in DataFlowEdge
description: EDGE_STYLES[edgeType] lookup assumes edgeType is always a valid key. If edgeData.edgeType contains a value not in EDGE_STYLES (e.g., from a deserialized diagram with a newer edge type), style will be undefined, causing a runtime crash on style.stroke access. Add fallback: EDGE_STYLES[edgeType] ?? EDGE_STYLES.http.
acceptanceCriteria: Unknown edge types fall back to HTTP style instead of crashing
Deserialized diagrams with newer edge types render without error

title: Validate OAuth redirect URI against strict allowlist
description: OAuth callback redirect URI is not validated against an allowlist. Attacker can redirect users to malicious sites after authentication. Define strict allowlist of valid redirect URIs and validate server-side before redirecting. Configure Clerk with exact redirect URIs (no wildcards).
acceptanceCriteria: Redirect URI validated against allowlist before every OAuth redirect
Invalid redirect URIs return 400 error
Clerk configured with exact redirect URIs in dashboard

title: Sanitize SVG output during export to prevent injection
description: Exported SVG diagrams may contain injected JavaScript or malicious SVG elements. Strip all <script>, on* attributes, and <foreignObject> elements from SVG output. Serve exported SVGs with Content-Disposition: attachment header.
acceptanceCriteria: SVG exports strip all script tags and event handler attributes
SVG exports strip foreignObject elements
Downloaded SVGs include Content-Disposition: attachment header

title: Sanitize template and gallery markdown descriptions
description: Template and gallery descriptions rendered as markdown without sanitization allow HTML injection. Use react-markdown with rehype-sanitize plugin. Define allowed HTML elements and attributes. Strip all event handlers and script elements.
acceptanceCriteria: Gallery descriptions rendered via react-markdown with rehype-sanitize
Template descriptions rendered with sanitization
No raw HTML injection possible in community content

title: Prevent SSRF in OG image avatar URL fetching
description: OG image generation fetches avatar URLs server-side. Attacker can supply internal network URLs to scan/access internal services. Validate avatar URLs against domain allowlist. Block private IP ranges. Set request timeout and disable redirects.
acceptanceCriteria: Avatar URLs validated against domain allowlist before fetching
Private IP ranges (10.x, 172.16-31.x, 192.168.x, 127.x) blocked
Request timeout set to 5 seconds
Redirects validated against same allowlist

title: Validate postMessage origin on all window message handlers
description: window.postMessage listeners do not validate the origin of incoming messages. Any iframe or window can send commands. Add strict origin allowlist check on every message event handler. Validate message structure with Zod.
acceptanceCriteria: All message event handlers check event.origin against allowlist
Messages from unknown origins are rejected and logged
Message structure validated with Zod schema

title: Set WASM memory limits and watchdog timer
description: WASM modules can allocate unbounded memory causing browser tab crash. Set maximum memory pages in WASM instantiation. Implement watchdog timer that terminates long-running computations. Run WASM in Web Worker to isolate from main thread.
acceptanceCriteria: WASM instantiation sets maximum memory pages
Watchdog timer terminates computations exceeding 30 seconds
WASM runs in Web Worker, not main thread
User warned when approaching memory limits

title: Verify Inngest webhook signature on all incoming events
description: Inngest webhook endpoints do not verify INNGEST_SIGNING_KEY signature. Attacker can forge webhook payloads to trigger background jobs. Verify signing key on every incoming webhook. Reject invalid/missing signatures.
acceptanceCriteria: INNGEST_SIGNING_KEY verified on every webhook request
Requests with invalid signatures return 401
Signature verification failures logged for monitoring

title: Add CSRF protection to all state-changing Route Handlers
description: Route Handlers accepting POST/PUT/DELETE do not validate Origin header, making them vulnerable to CSRF. Validate Origin header matches allowed origins on all mutation requests. Use SameSite=Strict cookies.
acceptanceCriteria: All POST/PUT/DELETE route handlers validate Origin header
Mismatched origins return 403 Forbidden
Cookies set with SameSite=Strict attribute

title: Prevent account enumeration via generic error messages
description: Clerk Frontend API may return different responses for existing vs non-existing accounts. Configure Clerk to return generic error messages. Rate limit sign-in/sign-up endpoints. Add CAPTCHA after failed attempts.
acceptanceCriteria: Sign-in errors return generic 'Invalid credentials' message
Sign-up errors do not reveal if email is already registered
Rate limiting: 5 attempts per 15 minutes per IP

title: Prevent WebSocket message flooding with per-connection rate limits
description: No per-connection rate limiting on WebSocket messages. A single client can flood the server, degrading service for all users. Implement per-connection message rate limiting (100 messages/second) and bandwidth limit. Disconnect clients exceeding limits.
acceptanceCriteria: Per-connection message rate limit of 100 messages/second
Per-connection bandwidth limit enforced
Clients exceeding limits are disconnected with warning
Rate limit violations logged

title: Implement AI data minimization for GDPR compliance
description: Full diagram data including potentially sensitive labels sent to Anthropic API without data minimization. Strip unnecessary fields before sending. Anonymize user identifiers. Send only structural data needed for evaluation.
acceptanceCriteria: User identifiers stripped/anonymized before AI API calls
Only structural diagram data sent (no personal info)
AI data processing documented in privacy policy

title: Use cryptographically secure random for encryption key generation
description: Encryption keys for shared diagrams may use Math.random() instead of crypto.getRandomValues(). Audit all key generation code and replace with Web Crypto API crypto.subtle.generateKey() for AES keys.
acceptanceCriteria: All encryption key generation uses crypto.getRandomValues() or crypto.subtle.generateKey()
Zero uses of Math.random() in security-sensitive code paths
Audit confirms no weak randomness in key generation

title: Add constant-time comparison and timing attack prevention for auth
description: Timing differences in authentication responses can reveal whether an account exists. Ensure constant-time comparison for all custom auth checks. Add artificial delay jitter to auth responses.
acceptanceCriteria: All token/key comparisons use constant-time algorithm
Auth response times have artificial jitter to prevent timing analysis
Custom auth endpoints return identical error format for all failures

title: Create push_subscriptions database table for web push notifications
description: User preferences schema includes push notification settings and the notifications system mentions web-push, but no table stores push subscription endpoints. Create push_subscriptions table with user_id, endpoint, p256dh_key, auth_key, created_at, expires_at.
acceptanceCriteria: push_subscriptions table created in Drizzle schema
Foreign key to users table with cascade delete
Unique constraint on (user_id, endpoint)
Migration generated and applied

title: Create email_sequences table for drip campaign tracking
description: MEGA_PROMPT specifies 5-email welcome drip over 7 days and re-engagement sequences. No table tracks which emails have been sent. Create email_sequences table with user_id, sequence_name, step_number, sent_at, opened_at, clicked_at.
acceptanceCriteria: email_sequences table created in Drizzle schema
Tracks per-user per-sequence step progress
Unique constraint on (user_id, sequence_name, step_number)
Migration generated and applied

title: Create feature_flags table for progressive rollout and A/B testing
description: Feature flags needed for progressive rollout of 12 modules and A/B testing. Create feature_flags table with name, enabled, percentage_rollout, user_segment, created_at, updated_at.
acceptanceCriteria: feature_flags table created in Drizzle schema
Supports boolean enable/disable and percentage-based rollout
Unique constraint on flag name
Helper function to check if flag is enabled for a user

title: Create learning_paths and learning_path_nodes tables
description: Screen 14 (Learning Path View) shows 5 preset paths + custom paths. No tables store path definitions or user path progress. Create learning_paths, learning_path_nodes, and user_learning_paths tables.
acceptanceCriteria: learning_paths table with id, slug, title, description, is_preset, created_by_user_id
learning_path_nodes table with id, path_id, concept_id, position, sort_order
user_learning_paths table with user_id, path_id, started_at, completed_at
5 preset paths seeded

title: Implement Inngest re-engagement email function
description: Cron-triggered Inngest function that sends re-engagement emails to inactive users at 7-day, 14-day, and 30-day intervals. Each interval has a different email template with escalating urgency.
acceptanceCriteria: Inngest function triggers daily on cron schedule
Identifies users inactive for 7d, 14d, and 30d
Sends appropriate email template per inactivity tier
Respects email opt-out preferences

title: Implement Inngest daily analytics rollup function
description: Cron-triggered Inngest function that aggregates activity_events into daily stats for dashboard and admin reporting. Computes daily active users, challenge completions, module usage, and community engagement metrics.
acceptanceCriteria: Inngest function triggers daily at midnight UTC
Aggregates activity_events into daily_stats table or JSON
Computes DAU, challenge completions, module usage distribution
Results available for admin dashboard

title: Build Home Dashboard page with progress, recent work, and recommendations
description: Create /dashboard route with WelcomeBanner (greeting, streak, quick-actions), ProgressSummaryRow (4 stat cards), RecentDesigns list (5 cards with thumbnails), AIRecommendations panel, and StreakActivitySection (GitHub-style heatmap). Include skeleton loading states and responsive layouts.
acceptanceCriteria: Route /dashboard renders dashboard page
WelcomeBanner shows greeting and 4 quick-action buttons
ProgressSummaryRow shows Level/XP, Streak, Modules, Interview stats
RecentDesigns shows up to 5 recent designs with thumbnails
Skeleton loading states for all sections
Responsive: tablet 2x2, mobile stacked

title: Build Module Selection grid page with filter, sort, and progress
description: Create /modules route with page header filter row (Difficulty, Status, Sort), progress overview bar, and 12-card module grid (360x320px cards with gradient backgrounds, progress bars, difficulty badges, prerequisite lock overlays). Include filter/sort logic and skeleton loading.
acceptanceCriteria: Route /modules renders module selection grid
12 module cards displayed in responsive grid
Filter by difficulty and completion status
Sort by name, difficulty, or progress
Progress bar on each card reflects user progress
Locked modules show prerequisite overlay

title: Refactor large module files into per-panel sub-components
description: Split DistributedModule.tsx (775 lines), OSModule.tsx (808 lines), ConcurrencyModule.tsx (663 lines), NetworkingModule.tsx (625 lines), InterviewModule.tsx (484 lines), and AlgorithmModule.tsx (439 lines) into separate files per panel (sidebar, canvas, properties, bottom). Each module should be composed from 4-5 smaller files.
acceptanceCriteria: Each module file is under 200 lines
Sidebar, canvas, properties, and bottom panel are separate components
No functional regressions after split
All existing tests (when written) still pass
